---
title: "The_Crown_report"
author: "Yuliya Martyniuk"
date: "2026-04-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
```

# The Crown

## Description

*The Crown* is a historical drama television series about the reign of Queen Elizabeth II, created and principally written by Peter Morgan and produced by Left Bank Pictures and Sony Pictures Television for Netflix. Morgan developed the series from his film The Queen (2006) and his stage play The Audience (2013), which also focused on Elizabeth.

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.align='center', out.width='30%'}
knitr::include_graphics("crown_photo.jpg")
```

## Some statistics 

```{r, echo=FALSE}
#  IMDb ratings as the 'Ratings' metric
crown_stats <- data.frame(
  Season = rep(1:6, each = 10),
  Episode = 1:60,
  Rating = c(
    # Season 1
    8.2, 8.9, 8.1, 8.6, 8.5, 8.0, 8.7, 8.3, 9.0, 8.5,
    # Season 2
    8.1, 8.1, 8.3, 8.5, 8.9, 9.1, 8.1, 9.1, 8.7, 8.3,
    # Season 3
    7.9, 8.4, 9.3, 8.6, 8.0, 8.9, 8.6, 8.2, 8.4, 8.8,
    # Season 4 
    8.1, 8.6, 8.9, 8.3, 8.5, 8.7, 8.2, 8.8, 9.1, 8.5,
    # Season 5 
    7.5, 7.8, 8.0, 7.3, 7.6, 7.9, 7.2, 7.7, 7.4, 7.5,
    # Season 6
    7.4, 7.6, 8.1, 8.5, 7.2, 7.5, 7.8, 8.3, 7.9, 8.4
  )
)

# Summary statistics
summary_stats <- data.frame(
  Mean_Rating = mean(crown_stats$Rating),
  Median_Rating = median(crown_stats$Rating),
  Highest_Rating = max(crown_stats$Rating),
  Lowest_Rating = min(crown_stats$Rating)
)

print(summary_stats)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(crown_stats, aes(x = Episode, y = Rating)) +
  geom_line(color = "#B1954C", size = 1) + # Royal Gold color
  geom_point(aes(color = factor(Season))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "The Crown: Episode Ratings Over Time (Seasons 1-6)",
       subtitle = "Data source: IMDb / Wikipedia",
       x = "Episode Number (Overall)",
       y = "Viewer Rating",
       color = "Season") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 60, by = 10))
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
crown_stats$Change <- c(0, diff(crown_stats$Rating))

ggplot(crown_stats, aes(x = Episode, y = Change, fill = Change > 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("firebrick", "forestgreen"), 
                    labels = c("Decrease", "Increase"),
                    name = "Rating Change") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Episode-to-Episode Rating Fluctuation",
       x = "Episode Number",
       y = "Change in Rating Points")
```
